hero.headline: Sharda Online MBA in Finance: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A+ | NIRF #87 University | UGC-DEB Approved | WES Recognised | Total all-in ₹1,54,500

sections.tldr: Sharda Online MBA in Finance covers Direct and Indirect Taxes, Financial Derivatives and Risk Management, Fundamentals of Fintech, Mergers Acquisitions and Corporate Restructuring, Security Analysis and Portfolio Management, and International Financial Management across Sem 3-4. Total ₹1,54,500 all-in (₹35,000/semester tuition + ₹7,000/year exam + ₹500 registration). No scholarships. No bundled perks. NAAC A+, NIRF #87, UGC-DEB approved.

about.body:
Sharda University, Greater Noida offers an Online MBA with Finance specialisation. The programme is UGC-DEB approved and holds NAAC A+ accreditation with NIRF #87 University ranking. WES recognised for international credential evaluation.

The Finance specialisation covers six dedicated subjects across Sem 3-4: Direct and Indirect Taxes, Financial Derivatives and Risk Management, Fundamentals of Fintech in Sem 3, and Mergers Acquisitions and Corporate Restructuring, Security Analysis and Portfolio Management, International Financial Management in Sem 4. Common subjects include Entrepreneurship and Startup Ideation, Production and Operations Management, Cross Functional Elective (Sem 3), Strategic Management, and Research Project (Sem 4).

The curriculum spans traditional finance (tax, portfolio management, M&A) and modern finance (fintech, derivatives). At ₹1,54,500 all-in, Sharda is positioned between budget NCR options (NIU ₹97,600, Galgotias ₹86,400) and premium programmes (Amity ₹2,25,000, NMIMS ₹1,96,000+). The NAAC A+ and NIRF rank provide accreditation credibility, but brand recognition in the online MBA finance space is still developing.

EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm interest structure with admissions.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Finance specialisation subjects alongside common subjects.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship and Startup Ideation
subjects.desc: Business ideation frameworks, lean startup methodology, business model canvas, venture feasibility, and funding landscape for new ventures.

subjects.name: Production and Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning, inventory management, quality control, lean principles, and operations strategy for manufacturing and service sectors.

subjects.name: Cross Functional Elective
subjects.desc: Elective subject from another specialisation domain. Broadens management perspective beyond the core finance track.

subjects.name: Direct and Indirect Taxes
subjects.desc: Indian income tax provisions, GST framework, TDS/TCS, corporate tax planning, and compliance requirements for businesses operating in India.

subjects.name: Financial Derivatives and Risk Management
subjects.desc: Options, futures, swaps, hedging strategies, derivative pricing models, credit risk assessment, and enterprise risk management frameworks.

subjects.name: Fundamentals of Fintech
subjects.desc: Digital payments ecosystem, blockchain basics, peer-to-peer lending models, robo-advisory platforms, InsurTech, and India's fintech regulatory landscape.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Corporate strategy, competitive advantage frameworks, strategic planning models, and business model analysis.

subjects.name: Research Project
subjects.desc: Faculty-supervised research project applying finance concepts and analytical methods to a real business or market problem.

subjects.name: Mergers Acquisitions and Corporate Restructuring
subjects.desc: M&A valuation methods (DCF, comparable, precedent), due diligence, deal structuring, hostile takeovers, and post-merger integration frameworks.

subjects.name: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management
subjects.desc: Fundamental and technical analysis, equity valuation, portfolio construction, asset allocation, Modern Portfolio Theory, and risk-adjusted performance measurement.

subjects.name: International Financial Management
subjects.desc: Foreign exchange markets, currency risk management, international capital budgeting, cross-border M&A, and global financial regulation basics.

whoHires.body:
Finance MBA graduates target roles across banking, investment, and corporate finance:

**Industries:** Banking and financial services, insurance, asset management, fintech, consulting, corporate treasury, and NBFC operations

**Entry-level roles:** Financial Analyst, Credit Analyst, Investment Banking Associate, Tax Consultant, Treasury Analyst, Equity Research Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Finance Manager, Senior Financial Analyst, Portfolio Manager, Risk Manager, M&A Associate, FP&A Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CFO, VP Finance, Head of Risk, Director of Financial Planning, Chief Investment Officer, Head of M&A

**Employers:** Banks (HDFC, ICICI, Axis, Kotak), consulting (Deloitte, EY, KPMG, PwC financial advisory), insurance (HDFC Life, ICICI Prudential, SBI Life), AMCs (SBI MF, HDFC AMC, Nippon), and fintech (Razorpay, PhonePe, Paytm financial services).

**Note:** Sharda provides career support. Greater Noida location offers NCR recruiter access, but finance roles at top banks and investment firms will weigh brand alongside credentials.

skills.body:
The Finance specialisation builds skills tested by BFSI, corporate finance, and advisory employers:

**Technical skills:**
- Tax: Indian income tax and GST compliance, corporate tax planning, TDS/TCS provisions, and tax advisory for business entities
- Derivatives and Risk: option and futures pricing, hedging strategy design, credit risk assessment, and enterprise risk framework application
- Security Analysis: equity valuation (DCF, multiples), technical chart analysis, portfolio construction, and risk-adjusted return measurement
- M&A: deal valuation, due diligence checklist management, deal structuring, and post-merger integration planning

**Applied skills:**
- Fintech: digital payment system architecture, blockchain use cases in banking, and fintech business model evaluation
- International Finance: forex risk management, cross-border capital budgeting, and international trade finance basics

**Soft skills:** Financial modelling presentation to leadership, investor communication, regulatory compliance reporting, credit committee presentation, and cross-functional finance-operations collaboration.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Sharda Online MBA Finance against peer programmes:

**vs NIU Online MBA Finance (₹97,600):** NIU is ₹56,900 cheaper with 10 dedicated finance subjects including Blockchain and Financial Planning as standalone subjects. Both are NCR-based with NAAC A+. Sharda has NIRF #87 ranking. NIU lacks NIRF ranking. Budget-conscious candidates should seriously consider NIU.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA Finance (₹86,400):** Galgotias is the cheapest NCR option at ₹68,100 less than Sharda, with a Master Thesis component. Sharda adds Financial Derivatives, Fintech, and International Financial Management as dedicated subjects. Galgotias wins on fee. Sharda wins on subject depth and NIRF rank.

**vs Amity Online MBA Finance (₹2,25,000):** Amity at NIRF #22 with 18 specialisations and a large pan-India alumni network costs ₹70,500 more. For BFSI hiring where brand premium matters at large banks and AMCs, Amity justifies the price gap. Sharda is a mid-tier alternative.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Finance (₹1,96,000-₹2,20,000):** NMIMS carries strong Mumbai financial services brand recognition. At ₹40,000-₹65,000 more than Sharda, the brand premium is significant for investment banking and equity research hiring. Sharda is a cost-effective alternative without the brand equity.

**vs DPU Online MBA Finance (₹1,89,400):** DPU at NIRF #41 with NAAC A++ and edX partnership is ₹34,900 more than Sharda. DPU wins on accreditation grade and western India brand. Sharda wins on fee for NCR-based professionals.

fees.body:
The Sharda Online MBA in Finance has a total all-in cost of ₹1,54,500.

**Fee breakdown:**
- Tuition: ₹1,40,000 (₹35,000 per semester x 4)
- Exam fees: ₹14,000 (₹7,000 per year x 2)
- Registration fee: ₹500 (one-time, same as application fee)
- **Total all-in: ₹1,54,500**

**EMI:** ₹7,375/month. EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm interest structure with admissions.

**Scholarships:** Sharda online MBA does not advertise standing scholarship categories. Check with admissions for any admission-cycle incentives.

**Bundled perks:** None.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Sharda each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Finance as the specialisation, issued by Sharda University under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #87 University rank, UGC-DEB approval, and WES recognition. Valid for BFSI hiring, PSU applications, and higher education admission. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Sharda Online MBA Finance students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Kunal R.
items.liked: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management was well-structured. The derivatives subject covered practical hedging scenarios, not just Black-Scholes theory
items.disliked: No scholarships at all. At ₹1,54,500, NIU at ₹97,600 offers similar finance subjects for significantly less
items.body: The finance curriculum covers the right mix of traditional and modern topics. Derivatives, M&A, and Security Analysis are strong subjects. Fintech is introductory but relevant. At ₹1,54,500 the fee is fair given NAAC A+ and NIRF rank, but NIU and Galgotias offer comparable finance depth at lower prices. I chose Sharda for the NIRF ranking, which helped during my internal promotion review.

items.name: Deepa T.
items.liked: Direct and Indirect Taxes subject was practical. International Financial Management covered forex risk in a way I could apply to my export finance role
items.disliked: EMI interest was not zero-cost. The admissions team did not make this clear upfront. Confirm before enrolling
items.body: The tax and international finance subjects were the strongest for my banking career. M&A was solid on valuation methods. The fintech subject was basic. Brand recognition is a limitation. At large banks, Sharda's online MBA is not as readily recognised as Amity or NMIMS. The NAAC A+ and NIRF rank helped during the verification stage with my employer.

items.name: Manish P.
items.liked: NAAC A+ and NIRF #87 gave me confidence in the degree's validity. The M&A subject was directly relevant to my corporate finance work
items.disliked: Career services for online students are minimal. The placement support is focused on campus students in Greater Noida
items.body: I evaluated Sharda against DPU and JIIT. Sharda's ₹1,54,500 was the lowest of the three with comparable accreditation. The finance subjects are standard but well-delivered. No perks, no scholarships, no industry certifications bundled. The value is in the degree and accreditation, not the extras. Self-driven job search is necessary.

faqs.question: What finance subjects does Sharda Online MBA Finance cover?
faqs.answer: Six dedicated finance subjects: Direct and Indirect Taxes, Financial Derivatives and Risk Management, Fundamentals of Fintech (Sem 3), Mergers Acquisitions and Corporate Restructuring, Security Analysis and Portfolio Management, International Financial Management (Sem 4).

faqs.question: What is the total all-in fee for Sharda Online MBA Finance?
faqs.answer: ₹1,54,500 (₹1,40,000 tuition + ₹14,000 exam + ₹500 registration). EMI from ₹7,375/month through financing partners. Confirm interest rate. No scholarships, no bundled perks.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover fintech?
faqs.answer: Yes. Fundamentals of Fintech is a Sem 3 subject covering digital payments, blockchain basics, peer-to-peer lending, robo-advisory, and India's fintech regulatory landscape.

faqs.question: How does Sharda Finance compare to NIU Finance on fee?
faqs.answer: NIU at ₹97,600 is ₹56,900 cheaper with 10 dedicated finance subjects. Both have NAAC A+. Sharda has NIRF #87 ranking which NIU lacks. Choose based on whether the NIRF rank justifies the ₹56,900 premium for your career goals.

faqs.question: Is EMI zero-cost for Sharda Online MBA?
faqs.answer: EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm the interest rate structure directly with Sharda admissions before committing.

chooseIf: You want an MBA Finance with derivatives, M&A, tax, and fintech subjects backed by NAAC A+ and NIRF #87.
₹1,54,500 all-in fits your budget for a mid-tier finance MBA with credible accreditation.
NIRF ranking and NCR recruiter access matter for your banking or corporate finance career.
You want International Financial Management as a dedicated subject alongside domestic finance.
consider: Brand recognition is a gap for BFSI hiring at large banks. Amity (NIRF #22) and NMIMS have stronger finance MBA positioning.
Budget is the priority. NIU at ₹97,600 or Galgotias at ₹86,400 offer finance specialisations at significantly lower fees with NAAC A+.
No scholarships and no perks. No industry certifications bundled.
EMI interest rate may not be zero-cost. Confirm before committing.
